episode_summary: Guest Greg Foot joins the team for four facts spanning baby-calming car vibrations, a 17th‑century vacuum vs. horses demo, volcanoes that partly scrub methane, and Paul McCartney’s eye yoga. Along the way they discuss baby-cry science and white noise, how vacuums work and the LHC, volcanic eruptions and risks, and the history and claims of yoga.

facts.fact: Tests have shown that a Volvo 240 used to be the best car with the right vibrations to calm down a crying baby.
My fact this week is in 1654 a public competition was held between a team of 30 horses and a vacuum.
And my fact this week is that volcanoes sometimes clean up after themselves.
My fact this week is that Paul McCartney does eye yoga.
